split_recommendations.reason: High topic entropy (0.97) — document covers many disparate topics
suggested_titles: ✅Review their SMART Goal Progress Tracker – Ask your child to explain their goal, why it matters
✅Ask reflective questions – Help your child think through their plan by asking questions like:
Set a family goal together – Consider creating a shared SMART goal, like saving for a special
Celebrate progress! – Recognize their hard work by celebrating milestones, such as completing a
Grades 4-5 Financial Literacy Lesson 2 Handout A: “SMART Goals at Home Letter”

split_recommendations.source_file: 4-5.FL.2 Handout B. SMART Goal Brainstorm and Planning.pdf
split_recommendations.reason: High topic entropy (0.93) — document covers many disparate topics
suggested_titles: Part 1: Two Goals
Part 2: SMART Goals
Specific: Read chapter books Measurable: Read 4 chapter books
Achievable: I usually read 2 or 3 books per month. Increasing this to 4 books is realistic for me.
Relevant: I like reading chapter books so this is relevant to me.
Time-based: Read 4 chapter books per month for 6 months.

split_recommendations.reason: High topic entropy (0.98) — document covers many disparate topics
suggested_titles: SMART Goal:
Grades 4-5 Financial Literacy Lesson 2 Handout C: “SMART Goal Progress Tracker”
Date Specific Task/Step Progress (Completed/In Progress/ Not Started) Comments Final Reflection
1. Did I achieve my SMART goals? Why or why not?
2. What did I learn from this experience?
3. How will I use what I learned in the future?
